title: Multibit `reg` crashes Yosys without an error
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.31+49 (git sha1 e0ba07aed, x86_64-apple-darwin20.2-clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

macOS

### Reproduction Steps

Try to `read_verilog -sv` on the following file:

```verilog
module m(input [1:0] in, output [1:0] out);
  buf buf0[1:0] (out, in);
endmodule
```

### Expected Behavior

No crash.

### Actual Behavior

```

 /----------------------------------------------------------------------------\

 |                                                                            |
 \----------------------------------------------------------------------------/

 Yosys 0.31+49 (git sha1 e0ba07aed, x86_64-apple-darwin20.2-clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)


yosys> read_verilog -sv tmp.v 
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: tmp.v
Parsing SystemVerilog input from `tmp.v' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\m'.
libc++abi: terminating with uncaught exception of type std::out_of_range: vector
[1]    52823 abort      yosys
```
